methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $600M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $800M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Profluent, a Series B-stage biotech company, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector. The target's last post-money valuation stands at $600M, with $150M raised to date.

Two valuation methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.1B. Peer-median anchoring uses four same-stage biotech peers—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), and Asimov ($800M)—which produce a median of $1.3B and a range of $800M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples was inapplicable, as Profluent's revenue is not disclosed. The three public reference tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) showed a sector median P/S of 7.8x, but cannot be deployed without revenue data.

Triangulation across the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $969M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $1.2B. This implies a modest uplift from the current $600M post-money, consistent with Series B progression. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion across peer valuations ($800M to $3.3B) and the absence of revenue disclosure, which limits income-based cross-validation. Near-term financing metrics and clinical or technical milestones will materially narrow this range.
